qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: File-sharing example panics on request-response inbound failures after a remote disconnect
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a request-response example panic caused by an unhandled InboundFailure(ConnectionClosed) event in examples/file-sharing/src/network.rs, not the advisory's Gossipsub PRUNE.backoff integer/time overflow in libp2p-gossipsub backoff handling.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only qualifying finding concerns the file-sharing example's request-response event handling after a remote disconnect, while the advisory concerns unchecked Gossipsub PRUNE backoff arithmetic leading to a panic.
evidence: The advisory describes attacker-controlled Gossipsub ControlPrune backoff values reaching unchecked Duration/Instant arithmetic in libp2p-gossipsub and panicking the networking state machine.

The only qualifying finding describes request_response::Event::InboundFailure(ConnectionClosed) falling through a catch-all panic in examples/file-sharing/src/network.rs after a peer disconnects during a file response.
These reports do not share the vulnerable subsystem, input type, code path, or root-cause mechanism: Gossipsub backoff overflow versus request-response example event handling.
